sourceNotes: Historical-inspired, fictionalized for gameplay; does not use real party names in playable text.
Anchors include 2001 West Bengal Assembly election, Keshpur/Garbeta-style political violence, 2003 panchayat election violence/results, and 2004 Lok Sabha election dynamics.

defaults.reactionRule: Each news item contains its own 3 active reactions plus no_comment. Reaction weights and effects vary by issue profile.
defaults.thirdPartyReactionRule: THIRD_PARTY can use opposition-compatible and neutral reactions.

newsItems.title: Rural Clashes Put Cadre Politics Back In The Headlines
newsItems.description: Reports of clashes between rival local workers in western districts revive debate over intimidation and booth-level dominance.

reactionOptions.text: Call a peace march with civil society and local elders.
Demand immediate police action against violent cadres.

risk.badOutcome: Demand is seen as selective outrage.

reactionOptions.text: Announce a district security review and compensation process.
No comment.

balanceNotes.publicSupportScale: News reactions now use a wider -5..+5 support range. Extreme reactions can produce +5 upside but carry -5 backlash risk; no-comment usually costs -1 support/media.
balanceNotes.newsToneScale: Constructive/investigative reactions now usually give +4, mobilizing reactions can give +5 with -5 backlash risk, blame/attack reactions can visibly cost -3 support, and no-comment costs -2.
